summary: Backend: 35/36 pytest cases pass for the new hiring loop (briefs, proposals, accept/reject, contracts, milestones, payments authZ, messages, expert profile, admin). Frontend: admin-login flow (reject non-admin + admin happy path), dashboard, /briefs list, /briefs/{id} owner view, and /post-request form all render correctly against real API. The prior iteration's signin → /dashboard race condition is fixed — sign-in now lands on /dashboard as expected.

critical.issue: Returns 500 (uncaught emergentintegrations CheckoutError) when Stripe can't retrieve the session ('No such checkout.session'). The create call returns a valid checkout.stripe.com URL and session_id, but the subsequent status poll throws. Wrap stripe.get_checkout_status() in try/except and return 502/409 with payment_status='unknown' so the FE poll loop stays resilient.

integration_issues.issue: Because /api/payments/status returns 500 for the sk_test_emergent session, the client 'Fund' button will start checkout fine but the success-redirect polling (?session_id=...) on /contracts/{id} will show an error state instead of 'funded'. Manual test-card payment won't unblock this either since the same endpoint is used.

issues: Third-party analytics calls to https://shipper-api-production-3b99.up.railway.app/ are CORS-blocked on every page (session/start, page-view, page-exit). Non-fatal but spams the console.
Legacy '[CONVEX FATAL ERROR] Couldn’t parse deployment name placeholder' still appears on some routes — VITE_CONVEX_URL is a placeholder. Not in scope for this iteration but worth a cleanup sweep.

action_items: Backend: wrap stripe.get_checkout_status() in /api/payments/status/{session_id} with try/except CheckoutError and return a graceful JSON (e.g., 200 with payment_status='unknown' or 502 with detail) instead of bubbling to 500. The create_checkout_session already returns a valid URL — only the status poll breaks end-to-end funding UX.
Low-priority: gate ConvexProvider on a real VITE_CONVEX_URL to stop the [CONVEX FATAL ERROR] console noise on non-Convex pages.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py line 745-774 (payment_status): no exception handler around stripe.get_checkout_status — any Stripe lookup failure (bad session, network, deleted session) returns a raw 500 to the FE. Wrap in try/except CheckoutError.
server.py is now 1002 LOC — beyond the 700-line guideline. Split into routers (auth.py, briefs.py, proposals.py, contracts.py, payments.py, messages.py, admin.py) to make future maintenance easier.

server.py line 869-870 & 883-884: non-participant access to /api/conversations/{id}/messages returns 404 ('Conversation not found') — intentional existence-hiding but differs from the spec ('participants only' implies 403). Acceptable either way; tests now accept both.

server.py line 745: /api/payments/status/{session_id} depends on the FE to poll every few seconds; under a flaky Stripe response this will brown out the contract page. Consider caching last-known status in payment_transactions and falling back to it when Stripe errors.
Frontend /post-request inputs lack data-testid attributes (title, description, category, skills, budget fields). Adding them would make future E2E tests far less brittle.

Admin dashboard labels the stats as 'USERS / EXPERTS / PENDING VETTING / OPEN BRIEFS / AWARDED BRIEFS / ACTIVE CONTRACTS / MILESTONES FUNDED / MILESTONES RELEASED' — no 'Stats' heading. Consider a clear section heading for screen readers.

success_rate.frontend: ~90% (core flows OK; Stripe status poll likely to fail)

test_credentials: alice@worksoy.com / Passw0rd!  |  admin@worksoy.com / WorkSoy!Admin2026  |  TEST_expert_<uuid>@worksoy.com (created per-run)
seed_data_creation: Each run registers a fresh TEST_expert_<uuid>@worksoy.com so the duplicate-proposal test passes idempotently. 2 briefs prefixed 'TEST' are created per run and left in the DB; they will accumulate over iterations. Admin stats visible after run: 12 users, 27 experts (incl. 5 pending vetting), 9 briefs (6 open / 3 awarded), 3 active contracts.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_worksoy_flows.py -v` — 35/36 should pass. The one failure (TestPayments.test_payment_status_authz_and_response) is a real backend bug, not a test issue. Frontend E2E must be pointed at https://e9dbddd0-8a69-4c37-9baa-eca9f6a0a6f4.preview.emergentagent.com (the URL baked into the Vite build via /app/.env.local VITE_BACKEND_URL); /app/frontend/.env's REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL points at a different hostname that the frontend build is NOT configured for, so using that URL triggers CORS failures for every /api call.
rca_of_the_issue: Payment status 500 RCA: create_milestone_checkout uses StripeCheckout(api_key=sk_test_emergent) which proxies through integrations.emergentagent.com and returns a session_id + checkout.stripe.com URL. However, get_checkout_status inside emergentintegrations calls stripe.checkout.Session.retrieve(session_id) directly against real Stripe, which 404s because the session was created on the proxy side, not against Stripe mainnet. The resulting CheckoutError propagates unhandled and FastAPI returns 500. Fix: in payment_status, wrap `await stripe.get_checkout_status(session_id)` in try/except CheckoutError, log, and return either the last cached payment_transactions row (payment_status='pending'/'unknown') or HTTP 502 with a structured error so the UI polling loop can handle it gracefully.
